explanation: The specification requires that each of the modes (represented by the atomic propositions prop_0 to prop_5) is possible at some point in the future, i.e., the system should allow each mode. In LTL, this is expressed as "eventually prop_0 holds, and eventually prop_1 holds, ..., and eventually prop_5 holds."

explanation: The requirement asks that the controller be able to implement (or use) the listed modes. explanation: The requirement states that the controller must implement all the listed modes, which translates to saying that, at some point in time, each atomic proposition will be true at least once (i.e., they are all eventually reachable). In LTL, this is expressed using the 'eventually' operator (F) for each mode, combined with conjunction (&).

explanation: The requirement is that the controller shall implement the modes, which we interpret as: at every moment in time, at least one mode (atomic proposition) holds. This is captured using the LTL globally operator (G), ensuring that at each point in time, at least one of prop_0 through prop_5 is true.

To express that the controller implements the listed modes, we need to assert that, at every moment, it is in exactly one of these modes. This is done by stating that at all times, one of the propositions is true, and no two of them are true at the same time.

To ensure that the controller implements the modes prop_0 through prop_5, we can specify that globally, at each point in time, at least one of the mode propositions holds (i.e., the controller is always in one of the allowed modes).

The requirement is that the system may at any time be in one of the specified modes, represented by the atomic propositions prop_0 through prop_5. To formalize this in LTL, we express that globally (always), at least one of the supported modes holds. We take the disjunction (OR) of all six propositions and require this globally.

The requirement states that the controller must implement all the given modes, which means that, at all times, it should be possible for each mode to eventually occur. In LTL, this is captured by requiring that each mode holds at least once somewhere along the timeline, using the 'eventually' (F) operator for each mode. We take the conjunction (AND) of 'eventually' for all modes to state that all are required at least once.
output_LTL: F (StartUpMode) & F (SelfTestMode) & F (StandbyMode) & F (PCVMode) & F (PSVMode) & F (failSafeMode)

explanation: The requirement is that the controller shall implement all six modes. This means that, at some point (possibly different for each mode), each mode should eventually be active (true). In LTL, this is expressed as the conjunction of 'eventually' for each atomic proposition.
